title: triticum aestivum var. turgidum
intro: Triticum aestivum var. turgidum, commonly known as durum wheat, is a type of wheat plant that is widely cultivated for its high protein content and hard, dense grains. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include its high fiber content and antioxidant activity. Consuming durum wheat has been associated with several health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced risk of heart disease, and lower cholesterol levels. Durum wheat can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, powder, and flour, which can be used in cooking and as a dietary supplement to support overall health and well-being.
